method.name: Constructor
method.description: Initializes a Ruckig online trajectory generator . The Ruckig online trajectory generator provides instantaneous trajectory generation capabilities for motion control systems. This function prepares a position-based trajectory generation object

param.name: params
param.description:
parameters of the function:
pos (vector n): specifies the current configuration with one value per DoF
vel (vector n, defaults to zero vector): optional. Specifies current velocity with one value per DoF
accel (vector n, defaults to zero vector): optional. Specifies current acceleration with one value per DoF
maxVel (vector n): specifies the maximum allowed velocity with one value per DoF
minVel (vector n, defaults to -maxVel): optional. Specifies the minimum allowed velocity with one value per DoF
maxAccel (vector n): specifies the maximum allowed acceleration with one value per DoF
minAccel (vector n, defaults to -maxAccel): optional. Specifies the minimum allowed acceleration with one value per DoF
maxJerk (vector n): specifies the maximum allowed jerk with one value per DoF
flags (int): optional. Ruckig flags , -1 for default flags.
targetPos (vector n): specifies the target configuration with one value per DoF
targetVel (vector n, defaults to zero vector): optional. Specifies the target velocity with one value per DoF
baseCycleTime (float, defaults to 0.0001): optional. Smallest expected cycle time
timeStep (float, defaults to 0.0): optional. The desired time step size. A value of 0 indicates that the current simulation time step is used. The timeStep time should always be a multiple of baseCycleTime
selection (int[n], defaults to [1, ..., 1]): optional

param.name: motionObject
param.description: a motion object

method.name: remove
method.description: Destroys the motion object

method.name: step
method.description: Steps the motion

param.name: result
param.description:
return value of the update function in the motion library:
1: Result::Finished (final state reached)
0: Result::Working (final state not yet reached)
-100: Result::ErrorInvalidInput
-101: Result::ErrorTrajectoryDuration
-110: Result::ErrorExecutionTimeCalculation
-111: Result::ErrorSynchronizationCalculation

method.name: data
method.description: Returns motion information

param.name: data
param.description: Motion information

maxAccel (vector n): specifies the maximum allowed acceleration with one value per DoF
minAccel (vector n, defaults to -maxAccel): optional. Specifies the minimum allowed acceleration with one value per DoF
maxJerk (vector n): specifies the maximum allowed jerk with one value per DoF
flags (int): optional. Ruckig flags , -1 for default flags.
targetVel (vector n, defaults to zero vector): optional. Specifies the target velocity with one value per DoF
baseCycleTime (float, defaults to 0.0001): optional. Smallest expected cycle time
timeStep (float, defaults to 0.0): optional. The desired time step size. A value of 0 indicates that the current simulation time step is used. The timeStep time should always be a multiple of baseCycleTime
selection (int[n], defaults to [1, ..., 1]): optional

param.name: motionObject

method.name: run
method.description: Executes the motion. Can only be called from scripts running in a thread, since this is a blocking operation

method.name: Constructor
method.description: Initiates a n-DoF motion using the Ruckig online trajectory generator

param.name: params
param.description:
parameters of the function:
pos (vector n): mandatory if joints is not specified. Specifies the current configuration with one value per DoF
joints (handle[]): mandatory if pos is not specified. Specifies the joint handles with one handle per DoF
vel (vector n, defaults to zero vector): optional. Specifies current velocity with one value per DoF
accel (vector n, defaults to zero vector): optional. Specifies current acceleration with one value per DoF
maxVel (vector n, defaults to infinity vector): optional. Specifies the maximum allowed velocity with one value per DoF
minVel (vector n, defaults to -maxVel): optional. Specifies the minimum allowed velocity with one value per DoF
maxAccel (vector n, defaults to infinity vector): optional. Specifies the maximum allowed acceleration with one value per DoF
minAccel (vector n, defaults to -maxAccel): optional. Specifies the minimum allowed acceleration with one value per DoF
maxJerk (vector n, defaults to infinity vector): optional. Specifies the maximum allowed jerk with one value per DoF
targetVel (vector n, defaults to zero vector): optional. Specifies the target velocity with one value per DoF
targetPos (vector n): specifies the target configuration with one value per DoF
flags (int): optional. Ruckig flags , -1 for default flags.
callback (function): optional. Callback function called for each motion step. The argument provided to the callback function is the current motion state
auxData (any): optional. Data to be forwarded to the callback function
cyclicJoints (bool[], defaults to [false, ..., false]): optional. Indicates which joint is cyclic (for cyclic joints, the movement always goes in the direction that represents the shortest distance to the goal)
timeStep (float, defaults to 0.0): optional. The desired time step size. A value of 0 indicates that the current simulation time step is used

param.name: motionObject
param.description: a motion object

method.name: step
method.description: Steps the motion

param.name: result
param.description:
step result:
negative: error
0: step successful, motion is still ongoing
1: step successful, motion ended
2: motion aborted

method.name: data
method.description: Returns motion information

param.name: data
param.description: Motion information

method.name: remove
method.description: Destroys the motion object

method.name: run
method.description: Executes a motion. Can only be called from a script running in a thread, since this is a blocking operation

method.name: Constructor
method.description: Initiates motion using the Ruckig online trajectory generator , by performing interpolations between two poses. The function can operate by handling 4 motion variables (x,y,z and angle between the two poses), or a single movement variable (t, which requires a metric to be specified for distance calculation between the two poses)

param.name: params
param.description:
parameters of the function:
pose (pose): mandatory if neither object nor ik are specified. Specifies current pose
object (handle): mandatory if neither pose nor ik are specified. Specifies the handle of the object to move
ik (map): mandatory if neither pose nor object are specified. Contains IK-relevant informations, if a kinematic chain should move via IK to a target:
tip (handle): specifies the tip object (on the end-effector)
target (handle): specifies the target object (the object to reach)
base (handle, defaults to nil): optional. Specifies the base object (the base of the kinematic chain)
joints (handle[], defaults to all joints within tip and base): optional. Specifies the handles of the joints involved in IK calculations
method (int, defaults to simIK.method_damped_least_squares): optional. Specifies the resolution method
damping (float, defaults to 0.02): optional. Specifies the resolution damping
iterations (int, defaults to 20): optional. Specifies the max. number of iterations
constraints (int, defaults to simIK.constraint_pose): optional. Specifies the constraints
precision (int[2], defaults to [0.001, 0.5 * math.pi / 180.0]): optional. Specifies the desired precision (linear and angular)
allowError (bool, defaults to false): optional. Specifies whether a resolution with precision values over what is allowed will be applied anyway
breakFlags (int, defaults to 0): optional. Specified the reasons-flags required for simIK.handleGroup to fail
targetPose (pose): specifies the target pose
targetVel (vector n, defaults to zero vector): optional. Specifies the target velocity, with n = 1 or n = 4
flags (int, default to -1): optional. Ruckig flags
metric (vector n, defaults to [1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 1.0]): optional. A metric to compute a pose-pose distance. With n = 4, the motion calculation happens in the 4-dimensional space (x, y, z, angle), with n = 1, it happens in the 1-dimensional space (t)
maxVel (vector n, defaults to infinity vector): optional. Specifies the maximum allowed velocity
minVel (vector n, defaults to -maxVel): optional. Specifies the minimum allowed velocity
maxAccel (vector n, defaults to infinity vector): optional. Specifies the maximum allowed acceleration
minAccel (vector n, defaults to -maxAccel): optional. Specifies the minimum allowed acceleration
maxJerk (vector n, defaults to infinity vector): optional. Specifies the maximum allowed jerk
callback (function): optional. Callback function called for each motion step. The argument provided to the callback function is the current motion state
auxData (any): optional. Data to be forwarded to the callback function
timeStep (float, defaults to 0.0): optional. The desired time step size. A value of 0 indicates that the current simulation time step is used

param.name: motionObject
param.description: a motion object

method.name: step
method.description: Steps the motion

param.name: result
param.description:
step result:
negative: error
0: step successful, motion is still ongoing
1: step successful, motion ended
2: motion aborted

method.name: data
method.description: Returns motion information

param.name: data
param.description: Motion information

method.name: remove
method.description: Destroys the motion object

method.name: generate
method.description: Generates a time optimal trajectory

param.name: params
param.description:
parameters of the function:
path (matrix n * dim): n path points/configurations, with dim dimensions per point
pathLengths (vector of size n): parameterization of the path. Each path point should have a corresponding length value (as the distance from the path's first point, along the path)
maxVel (vector of size dim): maximum allowed velocity, for each dimension/axis
minVel (vector of size dim, defaults to -maxVel): minimum allowed velocity, for each dimension/axis
maxAccel (vector of size dim): maximum allowed acceleration, for each dimension/axis
minAccel (vector of size dim, defaults to -maxAccel): minimum allowed acceleration, for each dimension/axis
samples (int, defaults to 1000): desired number of path points/configurations
boundaryCondition (string, defaults to "not-a-knot"): boundary condition

param.name: points
param.description: generated path

param.name: times
param.description: generated path point times

method.name: Constructor
method.description: Initializes a time optimal trajectory generator, based on the TOPPRA library

param.name: motionObject
param.description: a motion object

method.name: remove
method.description: Destroys the motion object

method.name: remove
method.description: Removes the console

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
noError (bool, defaults to false): errors are suppressed
delayed (bool, defaults to false): removal is delayed

method.description: Prints to the console

param.name: text
param.description: text to append

param.name: textColor
param.description: text color

method.description: Clears the console

method.name: remove
method.description: Removes the text editor

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
noError (bool, defaults to false): errors are suppressed
delayed (bool, defaults to false): removal is delayed

method.name: Constructor
method.description: Creates a path

param.name: ctrlPoints
param.description: the path control points, organized in dim * n matrix with n the number of path points, and dim the dimension of the path. Set to null for an empty path:

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
types (string[dim], defaults to ["lin", ..., "lin"]): allowed values are "lin" for cartesian coordinates, "ang" for revolute coordinates and "quat" for quaternions (where 4 consecutive values should be "quat")
metric (vector dim, defaults to [1.0, ..., 1.0]): a weight for each dimension
bounds (array[dim], defaults to [[], ..., []]): lower and upper bounds for each dimension. Revolute coordinates without bounds are cyclic
closed (bool, defaults to false): whether the path is closed or open
closedRepeatsStart (bool, defaults to false): whether a closed path has the first path point identical to the last path point
onlyCtrlPoints (bool, defaults to false): if true, then only the control points are taken into account (no interpolated path is generated)
ctrlPoints: optional map describing control point properties:
duplicateThreshold (float, defaults to 0.02): distance below which points are merged
linearityTolerance (float, defaults to 0.001): points within a certain distance to the segment formed by its two neighbours can be discarded
pointType (enum, defaults to "cube"): allowed values are "none", "sphere" and "cube"
pointColor (color): point color
pointRadius (float): point radius
lineType (enum, defaults to "line"): allowed values are "none", "line" and "tube"
lineColor (color): line color
tubeRadius (float): tube radius
showAxes (bool, defaults to false): whether points display reference frames
pathPoints: optional map describing path point properties:
type (enum, defaults to "quadraticBezier"): allowed values are "linear" and "quadraticBezier"
bezierSmoothing (float, defaults to 1.0): the Bezier smoothing strength
samplingDistance (float, defaults to 0.02): the maximum allowable spacing between consecutive points along the generated path
pointType (enum, defaults to "cube"): allowed values are "none", "sphere" and "cube"
pointColor (color): point color
pointRadius (float): point radius
lineType (enum, defaults to "line"): allowed values are "none", "line" and "tube"
lineColor (color): line color
tubeRadius (float): tube radius
showAxes (bool, defaults to false): whether points display reference frames

method.name: fromBuffer
method.description: Creates a path based on previously serialized path data

param.name: buffer
param.description: serialized path data

param.name: objects
param.description: objects that might be associated with the path, e.g. joint or sceneObjects.

method.name: fromJoints
method.description: Creates an empty path based on joints located between an end-effector and a base object (both not included, if of joint type). Ordering goes from base to end-effector

param.name: endEffector
param.description: end-effector object

param.name: base
param.description: base object. Can be null

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
metric (vector dim, defaults to [1.0, ..., 1.0]): a weight for each dimension
closed (bool, defaults to false): whether the path is closed or open
closedRepeatsStart (bool, defaults to false): whether a closed path has the first path point identical to the last path point
onlyCtrlPoints (bool, defaults to false): if true, then only the control points are taken into account (no interpolated path is generated)
ctrlPoints: optional map describing control point properties:
duplicateThreshold (float, defaults to 0.02): distance below which points are merged
linearityTolerance (float, defaults to 0.001): points within a certain distance to the segment formed by its two neighbours can be discarded
pointType (enum, defaults to "cube"): allowed values are "none", "sphere" and "cube"
pointColor (color): point color
pointRadius (float): point radius
lineType (enum, defaults to "line"): allowed values are "none", "line" and "tube"
lineColor (color): line color
tubeRadius (float): tube radius
showAxes (bool, defaults to false): whether points display reference frames
pathPoints: optional map describing path point properties:
type (enum, defaults to "quadraticBezier"): allowed values are "linear" and "quadraticBezier"
bezierSmoothing (float, defaults to 1.0): the Bezier smoothing strength
samplingDistance (float, defaults to 0.02): the maximum allowable spacing between consecutive points along the generated path
pointType (enum, defaults to "cube"): allowed values are "none", "sphere" and "cube"
pointColor (color): point color
pointRadius (float): point radius
lineType (enum, defaults to "line"): allowed values are "none", "line" and "tube"
lineColor (color): line color
tubeRadius (float): tube radius
showAxes (bool, defaults to false): whether points display reference frames

optional map:

onlyCtrlPoints (bool, defaults to false): if true, then only the control points are taken into account (no interpolated path is generated)
ctrlPoints: optional map describing control point properties:
duplicateThreshold (float, defaults to 0.02): distance below which points are merged
linearityTolerance (float, defaults to 0.001): points within a certain distance to the segment formed by its two neighbours can be discarded
pointType (enum, defaults to "cube"): allowed values are "none", "sphere" and "cube"
pointColor (color): point color
pointRadius (float): point radius
lineType (enum, defaults to "line"): allowed values are "none", "line" and "tube"
lineColor (color): line color
tubeRadius (float): tube radius
showAxes (bool, defaults to false): whether points display reference frames
pathPoints: optional map describing path point properties:
type (enum, defaults to "quadraticBezier"): allowed values are "linear" and "quadraticBezier"
bezierSmoothing (float, defaults to 1.0): the Bezier smoothing strength
samplingDistance (float, defaults to 0.02): the maximum allowable spacing between consecutive points along the generated path
pointType (enum, defaults to "cube"): allowed values are "none", "sphere" and "cube"
pointColor (color): point color

method.name: createMarkers
method.description: Creates markers allowing to visualize the path. Only joint-based paths, and paths with following coordinates can be visualized: ["lin", "lin", ...], ["lin", "lin", "lin", ...], ["lin", "lin", "lin", "quat", "quat", "quat", "quat", ...]

method.name: removeMarkers
method.description: Removes markers previously created with createMarkers()

method.name: distance
method.description: Returns the distance between two points

param.name: point1
param.description: point1. Must have the same dimension as the path

param.name: point2
param.description: point2. Must have the same dimension as the path

param.name: distance
param.description: distance between the two points

method.name: interpolate
method.description: Returns the linear interpolation between two points

param.name: point1
param.description: point1. Must have the same dimension as the path

param.name: point2
param.description: point2. Must have the same dimension as the path

param.name: t
param.description: interpolation factor

param.name: lerpPoint
param.description: interpolated point

method.name: configs
method.description: Return all equivalent configurations within the specified joint bounds (when a revolute coordinate's range exceeds 2π, the same physical pose can be represented by multiple angle values)

param.name: conf
param.description: configuration. Must have the same dimension as the path

param.name: confs
param.description: equivalent configurations, with one configuration per column

method.name: createShape
method.description: Creates an extrusion shape from the path

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
section (matrix 2 * n, defaults to transposed([[-0.01, -0.01], [0.01, -0.01], [0.01, 0.01], [-0.01, 0.01], [-0.01, -0.01]])): the extrusion profile, with x/y pairs. If the first and last pair overlap, the section is closed
upVector (vector3, defaults to [0.0, 0.0, 1.0]): up-vector, used to generate the extruded shape's orientation
axis (int, defaults to 0): indicates what up-vector to use:
0: the upVector above
1: the upVector is the x-axis of path points
2: the upVector is the y-axis of path points
3: the upVector is the z-axis of path points

method.name: closest
method.description: Returns the point on the path that is closest to the input point

param.name: point
param.description: input point. Must have the same dimension as the path

param.name: pointOnPath
param.description: closest point that lies on the path

param.name: t
param.description: the relative distance along the path to the output point

method.name: getPoint
method.description: Returns the point on the path that lies at a specific distance along the path

param.name: t
param.description: relative distance

param.name: pointOnPath
param.description: point on the path at relative distance t

method.name: Constructor
method.description: Creates an IK world. An IK world contains one or several IK groups, each one containing one or several IK elements

method.name: createGroup
method.description: Creates an IK group inside of the IK world. An IK group contains one or several IK elements, that will be solved together

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
method (string): the solving method. Allowed values are "pseudoInverse", "undampedPseudoInverse", "dampedLeastSquares" and "jacobianTranspose"
damping (float): the damping factor used with the damped least squares method
maxIterations (int): the maximum number of iterations the solver runs for this group
enabled (bool): whether the IK group is enabled

param.name: group
param.description: the created IK group object

method.name: solve
method.description: Sequentially solves all enabled IK groups contained in the IK world

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
debug (int, defaults to 0): the debug verbosity level
syncWorlds (bool, defaults to true): whether the IK world and the sim world should be synchronized with each other
ignoreTolerance (bool, defaults to false): whether precision/tolerance settings should be ignored when evaluating the result of the solve operation

param.name: result
param.description: the overall result

param.name: flags
param.description:
various flags:
IK.calc_notperformed: no calculation was performed
IK.calc_cannotinvert: cannot invert Jacobian matrix
IK.calc_invalidcallbackdata: invalid callback data
IK.calc_outoftolerance: the positional and orientational tolerance was overshot
IK.calc_largesteps: some joints moved larger steps than desired
IK.calc_boundshit: some joints had their bounds hit

param.name: precision
param.description: maximum positional and orientational deviation between tip/target pairs

method.name: syncFromSim
method.description: Synchronizes the IK world with the sim world: all object poses and joint configurations in the IK world are updated to reflect the current state of their sim world counterparts

method.name: syncToSim
method.description: Synchronizes the sim world with the IK world: all object poses and joint configurations in the sim world are updated to reflect the current state of their IK world counterparts

method.name: remove
method.description: Removes the IK world and releases all associated resources

method.name: addElementFromScene
method.description: Creates an IK element inside of this IK group. Items are imported from the sim world into the IK world

param.name: base
param.description: the base scene object of the kinematic chain. Can be null, in which case the world is the base

param.name: tip
param.description: the the tip scene object of the kinematic chain

param.name: target
param.description: the target scene object, i.e. the object the tip should follow / be constrained to

param.name: constraints
param.description:
the constraints the tip should try to satisfy relative to the target. Following flags are possible and can be combined:
IK.constraint_x: constraint along the base object's x-direction
IK.constraint_y: constraint along the base object's y-direction
IK.constraint_z: constraint along the base object's z-direction
IK.constraint_position: full positional constraint
IK.constraint_alphabeta: alpha-beta constraint
IK.constraint_gamma: gamma-angle constraint
IK.constraint_orientation: full orientational constraint
IK.constraint_pose: full positional and orientational constraint

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
precision (float[2]): the linear and angular precision (i.e. tolerance) for the IK element
weights (float[2], defaults to [1.0, 1.0]): the weights of the linear and angular components of the IK element
enabled (bool, defaults to true): whether the IK element is enabled

param.name: element
param.description: the created IK element object

method.name: solve
method.description: Solves the IK group, i.e. tries to satisfy the constraints of all enabled IK elements it contains

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
debug (int, defaults to 0): the debug verbosity level
syncWorlds (bool, defaults to true): whether the IK world and the sim world should be synchronized with each other
ignoreTolerance (bool, defaults to false): whether precision/tolerance settings should be ignored when evaluating the result of the solve operation

param.name: result
param.description: the overall result

param.name: flags
param.description:
various flags:
IK.calc_notperformed: no calculation was performed
IK.calc_cannotinvert: cannot invert Jacobian matrix
IK.calc_invalidcallbackdata: invalid callback data
IK.calc_outoftolerance: the positional and orientational tolerance was overshot
IK.calc_largesteps: some joints moved larger steps than desired
IK.calc_boundshit: some joints had their bounds hit

param.name: precision
param.description: maximum positional and orientational deviation between tip/target pairs

method.name: findConfig
method.description: Searches for one joint configurations that satisfy the provided target pose(s), for the IK element(s) contained in the IK group. Search happens in a randomized fashion

param.name: poses
param.description: one target pose for each enabled IK element contained in the IK group (at least one pose)

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
maxTime (float, defaults to 1.0): the maximum search time, in seconds
altConfigs (bool, defaults to false): whether alternate configurations (e.g. configurations that differ by +/- 360 degrees for non-cyclic revolute joints) should also be returned
orientationMetric (float, defaults to 0.5): the weight of the angular component (relative to the linear component) when computing pose-pose distances
solveDistance (float, defaults to 2.5): the pose-pose distance below which a full solve operation is attempted from the current random configuration

param.name: config
param.description: the found joint configuration(s), or null if no configuration was found within the allotted time. Configurations are the matrix' columns, with one value per joint (4 for spherical joints)

method.name: syncFromSim
method.description: Synchronizes the IK world with the sim world, limited to the objects involved in this IK group: object poses and joint configurations in the IK environment are updated to reflect the current state of their sim world counterparts

method.name: syncToSim
method.description: Synchronizes the sim world with the IK world, limited to the objects involved in this IK group: object poses and joint configurations in the sim world are updated to reflect the current state of their IK environment counterparts (e.g. after a solve operation)

method.name: setPose
method.description: Sets the pose of an IK object, in the IK world

param.name: pose
param.description: the desired pose

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
relativeToObject (IKObject, defaults to the world): indicates relative to which reference frame the pose is specified. Specify null (or omit) to set the absolute pose, or an IK object relative to whose reference frame the pose is specified

method.name: getPose
method.description: Retrieves the pose of an IK object, in the IK world

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
relativeToObject (IKObject, defaults to the world): indicates relative to which reference frame we want the pose. Specify null (or omit) to retrieve the absolute pose, or an IK object relative to whose reference frame we want the pose

param.name: pose
param.description: the pose

method.name: setPosition
method.description: Sets the position of an IK object, in the IK world

param.name: position
param.description: the position]

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
relativeToObject (IKObject, defaults to the world): indicates relative to which reference frame the position is specified. Specify null (or omit) to set the absolute position, or an IK object relative to whose reference frame the position is specified

method.name: getPosition
method.description: Retrieves the position of an IK object, in the IK world

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
relativeToObject (IKObject, defaults to the world): indicates relative to which reference frame we want the position. Specify null (or omit) to retrieve the absolute position, or an IK object relative to whose reference frame we want the position

param.name: position
param.description: the position

method.name: setQuaternion
method.description: Sets the orientation (as a quaternion) of an IK object, in the IK world

param.name: quaternion
param.description: the quaternion

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
relativeToObject (IKObject, defaults to the world): indicates relative to which reference frame the quaternion is specified. Specify null (or omit) to set the absolute orientation, or an IK object relative to whose reference frame the quaternion is specified

method.name: getQuaternion
method.description: Retrieves the orientation (as a quaternion) of an IK object, in the IK world

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
relativeToObject (IKObject, defaults to the world): indicates relative to which reference frame we want the quaternion. Specify null (or omit) to retrieve the absolute orientation, or an IK object relative to whose reference frame we want the quaternion

param.name: quaternion
param.description: the quaternion

param.description: File to open

method.name: fastIdleLoop
method.description: Speeds the idle loop up, for better reactivity when idle, i.e. not simulating

param.name: fast
param.description: enables/disable the fast idle loop. Calls to this method are cumulative, i.e. if the method was called n times with the argument enabled, it must be called n times with the argument disabled to fully disable the effect

method.name: throttle
method.description: Limits the rate at which a function can be called

param.name: function
param.description: The function to execute

param.name: interval
param.description: The throttle time

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
args (any[], defaults to []): arguments to pass to the function

method.name: scheduleExecution
method.description: Schedules a function to be called after a delay

param.name: function
param.description: The function to execute

param.name: delay
param.description: The execution delay

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
simulationTime (bool, defaults to false)
args (any[], defaults to []): arguments to pass to the function

param.name: handle
param.description: The handle to the function in the scheduler

method.name: cancelScheduledExecution
method.description: Cancels a scheduled function execution

param.name: handle
param.description: The handle of the scheduled function execution

param.name: result
param.description: The result of the operation

method.name: pack
method.description: Packs data via CoppeliaSim format. Functions and other non-serializable items are packed as string or None/nil values

param.name: data
param.description: data to be packed

param.name: packedData
param.description: packed data

method.name: unpack
method.description: Unpacks a buffer via CoppeliaSim format

param.name: packedData
param.description: packed data

param.name: data
param.description: unpacked data

method.name: packArray
method.description: Packs an array of numbers as little-endian

param.name: numbers
param.description: array of numbers. Non-numbers are packed as zeros

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
type (string, defaults to "double"): packing type. Allowed values are "double", "float", "int64", "int32", "uint32", "int16", "uint16", "int8" and "uint8"
start (int, defaults to 0): zero-based index from which on data is packed
count (int, defaults to 0): amount of doubles to pack (0 = all)

param.name: data
param.description: packed data

method.name: unpackArray
method.description: Unpacks a buffer into an array of numbers

param.name: data
param.description: packed data

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
type (string, defaults to "double"): packing type. Allowed values are "double", "float", "int64", "int32", "uint32", "int16", "uint16", "int8" and "uint8"
start (int, defaults to 0): zero-based index from which on data is unpacked
count (int, defaults to 0): amount of doubles to unpack (0 = all)
byteOffset (int, defaults to 0): byte offset to be added before reading the data

param.name: numbers
param.description: number array

method.name: serialize
method.description: Serializes data via CBOR format

param.name: data
param.description: input data

param.name: packedData
param.description: serialized data

method.name: deserialize
method.description: Deserializes a buffer via CBOR format

param.name: packedData
param.description: packed data

param.name: data
param.description: deserialized data

method.name: systemLock
method.description: Acquires or releases a system-wide semaphore, i.e. across several CoppeliaSim instances

param.name: key
param.description: the key

param.name: acquire
param.description: whether the semaphore should be acquired or released

method.name: quit
method.description: Triggers a quit signal for the application to end

method.name: logInfo
method.description: Outputs an information message in the status bar

param.name: message
param.description: the message. None/nil clears the status bar

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
undecorated (bool, defaults to false): an undecorated message is displayed
once (bool, defaults to false): subsequent calls with the same message are ignored

method.name: logWarn
method.description: Outputs a warning message in the status bar

param.name: message
param.description: the message. None/nil clears the status bar

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
undecorated (bool, defaults to false): an undecorated message is displayed
once (bool, defaults to false): subsequent calls with the same message are ignored

method.name: logError
method.description: Outputs an error message in the status bar

param.name: message
param.description: the message. None/nil clears the status bar

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
undecorated (bool, defaults to false): an undecorated message is displayed
once (bool, defaults to false): subsequent calls with the same message are ignored

method.name: createObject
method.info: same method present in both app and scene
method.description:
Creates one of following objects:
collection
detached script
console
textEditor
dummy
joint
camera
light
marker
custom scene object
shape
script
force sensor
proximity sensor
vision sensor
oc tree
point cloud
path
graph

param.name: params
param.description:
a map/dict describing the object to create. All fields are optional except for objectType. Fields described hereafter are not exhaustive (all writable properties of the object to create can be specified too):
collection fields:
type (string): "collection"
override (bool): collidable, measurable, detectable and visibility properties of scene objects are overridden
detached script fields:
type (string): "detachedScript"
detachedScript.type (string): the script type. As of now only "addon" is supported
language (string): the script language ("lua" or "python")
code (string): the script content
console fields:
type (string): "console"
title (string): console title
size.width (int): console width
size.height (int): console height
position.x (int): console position x
position.y (int): console position y
closeable (bool): whether there is a close button or not
resizable (bool): whether the console can be resized
hiddenInSimulation (bool): whether the console is hidden during simulation
fontSize (int): the size of the font
defaultColor (color): text default color
background (color): background color
sceneUid (int): the scene unique identifier (sim.scene.uid) to which this console belongs to. Set to -1 for visibility across the whole application
textEditor fields:
type (string): "textEditor"
title (string): editor title
size.width (int): editor width
size.height (int): editor height
position.x (int): editor position x
position.y (int): editor position y
closeable (bool): whether there is a close button or not
resizable (bool): whether the editor can be resized
hiddenInSimulation (bool): whether the editor is hidden during simulation
fontSize (int): the size of the font
defaultColor (color): text default color
background (color): background color
sceneUid (int): the scene unique identifier (sim.scene.uid) to which this editor belongs to. Set to -1 for visibility across the whole application
dummy fields:
type (string): "dummy"
size (float): size of the dummy
color.diffuse (color): diffuse color
color.specular (color): specular color
color.emission (color): emission color
camera fields:
type (string): "camera"
clippingPlanes (float[2]): camera near and far clipping planes
size (float): camera size
viewAngle (float): the view angle, for a camera operating in perspective projection mode
viewSize (float): the view size, for a camera operating in orthogonal projection mode
color.diffuse (color): diffuse color
color.specular (color): specular color
color.emission (color): emission color
light fields:
type (string): "light"
light.type (string): the light type, any of "omnidirectional", "directional" or "spot"
attenuationFactors (float[3]): constant, linear and quadratic attenuation factors
spotCutoffAngle (float): spotlight cutoff angle
size (float): light size
lightColor.diffuse (color): light diffuse color
lightColor.specular (color): light specular color
lightColor.emission (color): light emission color
color.diffuse (color): object diffuse color
color.specular (color): object specular color
color.emission (color): object emission color
marker fields:
type (string): "marker"
marker.type (string): the marker object type. Allowed values are "points", "lines", "tubes", "triangles", "spheres", "squares", "discs", "cubes", "cylinders", "axes" or "custom"
cyclic (bool): if the marker is full, then the first items are overwritten
local (bool): coordinate data is specified locally
overlay (bool): items are rendered on top of scene objects
itemSize (vector3): default item size
duplicateTolerance (float): indicates a distance threshold under which points are not added. Does not apply to line- or triangle items
itemCnt (int): maximum item count
itemColor (color): default item color
mesh.vertices (matrix 3 * n): vertices for a mesh item type (sim.markertype_custom)
mesh.indices (int[]): indices for a mesh item type (sim.markertype_custom). One zero-based index for each vertex
mesh.normals (matrix 3 * n): normals for a mesh item type (sim.markertype_custom). One normal for each vertex
custom scene object fields:
type (string): "customSceneObject"
size (float): size of the custom scene object
color.diffuse (color): diffuse color
color.specular (color): specular color
color.emission (color): emission color
joint fields:
type (string): "joint"
joint.type (string): the joint type. Allowed values are "revolute", "prismatic" or "spherical"
mode (int): the joint mode
dynamics.ctrlMode (enum): the joint dynamic control mode
bounds (float[]): joint lower and upper bounds, [] for cyclic revolute joints, or for spherical joints
screwLead (float): a revolute joint with a screw lead different than 0.0 is a screw
length (float): length of the joint
diameter (float): diameter of the joint
color.diffuse (color): diffuse color
color.specular (color): specular color
color.emission (color): emission color
shape fields:
type (string): "shape"
shadingAngle (float): the shading angle
culling (bool): whether the shape has its back faces culled
dynamic (bool): whether the shape is dynamically enabled
showEdges (bool): whether the shape appears with visible edges
color.diffuse (color): diffuse color
color.specular (color): specular color
color.emission (color): emission color
cube (map/dict): holds parameters for the creation of a cube:
size (int[3]): the size of the cube
rawMesh (bool): whether the shape is registered as a random mesh (i.e. not a primitive)
sphere (map/dict): holds parameters for the creation of a sphere:
radius (float): the radius of the sphere
rawMesh (bool): whether the shape is registered as a random mesh (i.e. not a primitive)
cylinder (map/dict): holds parameters for the creation of a cylinder:
radius (float): the radius of the cylinder
length (float): the length of the cylinder
open (bool): whether the cylinder is open
rawMesh (bool): whether the shape is registered as a random mesh (i.e. not a primitive)
cone (map/dict): holds parameters for the creation of a cone:
radius (float): the radius of the cone
height (float): the height of the cone
open (bool): whether the cone is open
rawMesh (bool): whether the shape is registered as a random mesh (i.e. not a primitive)
capsule (map/dict): holds parameters for the creation of a capsule:
radius (float): the radius of the capsule
length (float): the length of the capsule
rawMesh (bool): whether the shape is registered as a random mesh (i.e. not a primitive)
plane (map/dict): holds parameters for the creation of a plane:
size (int[2]): the size of the plane
rawMesh (bool): whether the shape is registered as a random mesh (i.e. not a primitive)
disc (map/dict): holds parameters for the creation of a disc:
radius (float): the radius of the disc
rawMesh (bool): whether the shape is registered as a random mesh (i.e. not a primitive)
heightField (map/dict): holds parameters for the creation of a height field:
heights (matrix): the heights
cellSize (float): the length/width of a cell
rawMesh (bool): whether the shape is registered as a random mesh (i.e. not a height field)
string (map/dict): holds parameters for the creation of a shape representing a text string:
text (string): the text
height (float): the text height
center (bool): whether the text is centered or not
mesh (map/dict): holds parameters for the creation of a mesh:
vertices (matrix 3 * n)
indices (int[]): the zero-based indices
normals (matrix 3 * 3n)
boundingBoxQuaternion (quaternion): the orientation of the shape's bounding box
frameOrigin (pose): the pose of the shape's frame
texture (map/dict): holds parameters for an optional texture:
resolution (int[2]): texture resolution
image (buffer): texture specified as rgb or rgba
rgba (bool): whether the texture is specified as rgba or rgb
coordinates (matrix 2 * m): texture coordinates
interpolate (bool): whether adjecent texture pixels are interpolated
decal (bool): whether the texture is applied as decal
horizFlip (bool): whether the texture is horizontally flipped
vertFlip (bool): whether the texture is vertically flipped
script fields:
type (string): "script"
script.type (string): the detached script type. Allowed values are "simulation" and "customization"
language (string): "lua" or "python"
code (string): the content of the script
scriptDisabled (bool): the script is initially disabled
force sensors fields:
type (string): "forceSensor"
size (float): size of the sensor
filterType (int): filter type (0 = average, 1 = median)
filterSampleSize (int): value count the filter operates on
consecutiveViolationsToTrigger (int): number of consecutive threshold violation for the sensor to trigger
forceThreshold (float): force threshold
torqueThreshold (float): torque threshold
color.diffuse (color): diffuse color
color.specular (color): specular color
color.emission (color): emission color
proximity sensor fields:
type (string): "proximitySensor"
proximitySensor.type (string): the sensor type. Allowed values are "cone", "pyramid", "cylinder", "disc" and "ray"
pointSize (float): the sensor point size
explicitHandling (bool): whether the sensor is explicitly handled
showVolume (bool): whether the detection volume is shown
frontFaceDetection (bool): whether front faces are detected
backFaceDetection (bool): whether back faces are detected
exactMode (bool): whether the sensor operates in exact or fast mode
randomizedDetection (bool): randomized detection (only with ray-type proximity sensors)
angleThreshold (float): threshold angle for limited angle detection
closeThreshold (float): smallest detection distance
volume_faces (int[2]): near and far face count for the detection volume
volume_subdivisions (int[2]): near and far subdivisions for the detection volume
volume_offset (float): offset for the detection volume
volume_range (float): range for the detection volume
volume_xSize (float[2]): near and far x size for the detection volume
volume_ySize (float[2]): near and far y size for the detection volume
volume_radius (float[2]): near and far radius for the detection volume
volume_angle (float): angle for the detection volume
color.diffuse (color): diffuse color
color.specular (color): specular color
color.emission (color): emission color
vision sensor fields:
type (string): "visionSensor"
explicitHandling (bool): whether the sensor is explicitly handled
showFrustum (bool): whether the view frustum is shown
useExtImage (bool): whether the sensor is passive (uses an external image)
resolution (int[2]): sensor resolution
clippingPlanes (float[2]): sensor near and far clipping planes
size (float): sensor size
viewAngle (float): the view angle, for a sensor operating in perspective projection mode
viewSize (float): the view size, for a sensor operating in orthogonal projection mode
backgroundColor (color): the sensor uses a specific color as default background (i.e. null pixels)
color.diffuse (color): diffuse color
color.specular (color): specular color
color.emission (color): emission color
oc tree fields:
type (string): "ocTree"
voxelSize (float): the voxel size
showPoints (bool): whether points should be drawn instead of voxels
pointSize (int): the point size in pixels, when voxels are rendered as points
point cloud fields:
type (string): "pointCloud"
cellSize (float): the maximum size of the OC tree voxels containing points
pointSize (int): the point size in pixels
maxPointsInCell (int): maximum number of points allowed in a same OC tree voxel
path fields:
type (string): "path"
ctrlPts (matrix 7 * n): control points, with a pose for each path point
hiddenDuringSim (bool): path is hidden during simulation
closed (bool): last control point connects to the first control point
orientationMetric (float): the weight of orientation vs position
ctrlPoints: optional map describing control point properties:
duplicateThreshold (float): distance below which points are merged
linearityTolerance (float): points within a certain distance to the segment formed by its two neighbours can be discarded
lineType (enum, defaults to "line"): allowed values are "none", "line" and "tube"
lineColor (color): line color
pathPoints: optional map describing path point properties:
type (enum, defaults to "quadraticBezier"): allowed values are "linear" and "quadraticBezier"
bezierSmoothing (float, defaults to 1.0): the Bezier smoothing strength
samplingDistance (float): the maximum allowable spacing between consecutive points along the generated path
pointType (enum): allowed values are "none", "sphere" and "cube"
pointColor (color): point color
pointRadius (float): point radius
lineType (enum, defaults to "line"): allowed values are "none", "line" and "tube"
lineColor (color): line color
showAxes (bool): whether points display reference frames
extrusion: optional map describing how extrusion shapes are generated:
section (matrix 2 * n): the extrusion profile, with x/y pairs. If the first and last pair overlap, the section is closed
color (color): shape color
upVector (vector3, defaults to [0.0, 0.0, 1.0]): up-vector, used to generate the extruded shape
axis (int, defaults to 0): indicates what up-vector to use:
0: the upVector above
1: the upVector is the x-axis of path points
2: the upVector is the y-axis of path points
3: the upVector is the z-axis of path points
dummy: optional map describing how control point dummies are displayed:
size (float): the dummy size
color (color): the dummy color
visible (bool): whether the dummy is visible
graph fields:
type (string): "graph"
bufferSize (int): buffer size
graphSize (float): size of the graph's scene object
backgroundColor (color): background color
foregroundColor (color): foreground color

param.name: object
param.description: the created object

method.name: handleMessagePump

param.name: count
param.description: number of called add-ons

method.name: saveImageToBuffer
method.description: Saves an image to a buffer. Depending on the provided image buffer and resolution, a greyscale, rgb or rgba image is written

param.description: image (in rgb, rgba or greyscale format)

param.name: resolution
param.description: x/y resolution of the provided image

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
format (string, defaults to "png"): image format, expressed via the file extension
quality (int, defaults to -1): quality of the image: 0 for best compression, 100 for best quality

param.name: imgBuffer
param.description: a buffer containing the image in packed format (e.g. png, jpg, etc.)

method.name: saveImage
method.description: Saves an image to file. Depending on the provided image buffer and resolution, a greyscale, rgb or rgba image is written

param.name: image
param.description: image (in rgb, rgba or greyscale format)

param.name: resolution
param.description: x/y resolution of the provided image

param.name: filename
param.description: name of the file to write. The file extension indicates the format

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
quality (int, defaults to -1): quality of the image: 0 for best compression, 100 for best quality

method.name: transformImage
method.description: Transforms an image in various ways (up/down scaling, vertical/horizontal flipping)

param.name: imageIn
param.description: input image

param.name: resolutionIn
param.description: resolution of the input image

param.name: desiredResolutionOut
param.description: desired resolution of the output image

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
type (string, defaults to "rgb"): the type of the returned image. Valid values are "rgb", "rgba" and "grey"
aspectRatio (string, defaults to "ignore"): "ignore" to ignore aspect ratio, "keep" to keep aspect ratio (the effective resolution of the returned image could be different), or "keepExpand" to keep aspect ratio by expanding (the effective resolution of the returned image could be different)
smooth (bool, defaults to true): whether adjacent pixels are interpolated
flipAxisX (bool, defaults to false): the image is flipped on its x-axis
flipAxisY (bool, defaults to false): the image is flipped on its y-axis

param.name: imageOut
param.description: output image

param.name: resolutionOut
param.description: output resolution

param.name: channels
param.description: nummer of bytes per pixel

method.name: transformBuffer
method.description: Modifies a buffer

param.name: inBuffer
param.description: input buffer

param.name: inFormat
param.description: input data format. Allowed is "uint8", "int8", "uint16", "int16", "uint32", "int32", "uint64", "int64", "float", "double", "rgb", "bgr", "rgba", "argb" and "base64"

param.name: outFormat
param.description: desired output data format. Allowed is "uint8", "int8", "uint16", "int16", "uint32", "int32", "uint64", "int64", "float", "double", "rgb", "bgr", "rgba", "argb" and "base64"

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
scale (float, defaults to 1.0): scaling factor
offset (float, defaults to 0.0): offset
clamp (bool, defaults to false): clamping

param.name: outBuffer
param.description: the modified buffer

method.name: setModuleEntry
method.description: Creates, modifies or destroys module menu entries. Those are user selectable items located in [Menu bar > Modules]. When selected, the corresponding script will have its sysCall_moduleEntry callback function triggered, or sim_message_eventcallback_moduleentry triggered

param.name: handle
param.description: handle of a module menu entry, or -1 to create one

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
label (string, defaults to ""): label of the module entry. If handle is -1, then a path can be specified, in order to build sub-menus, e.g. "Menu\nSubmenu1\nSubmenu2\nLabel"
enabled (bool, defaults to true): the item is enabled
checked (bool, defaults to false): the item is checked
checkable (bool, defaults to false): the item is checkable
remove (bool, defaults to false): the item will be removed

param.name: handle
param.description: the handle of the module menu entry

method.name: broadcast
method.description: Broadcasts a message to all scripts, except for the emitting script. Messages are received synchronously via the sysCall_msg callback function

param.name: message
param.description: message to broadcast, with following structure: {id --> "msgIdentifyingString", data --> {...}}

method.name: setEventFilters
method.description: Specifies filters for the sysCall_event callback, in this script

param.name: filters

method.name: wait
method.description: Waits for a certain amount of time

param.name: delay
param.description: minimum time duration to wait

param.name: simulationTime
param.description: whether waiting in simulation- or real-time

method.name: getObject
method.description: Retrieves an object in the current hierarchy tree, based on its path and name

param.name: objectPath
param.description: path and name of the object to find, or an object persistent unique id

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
noError (bool, defaults to false): generates a silent error if an object was not found
index (int, defaults to 0): fetches the n-th object (zero based index) that satisfies the specified objectPath

param.name: object
param.description: retrieved object

method.name: step
method.description: Triggers the next simulation step, when in stepping operation mode and running in a thread. When simulation is running, step() only returns once the simulation time has changed

method.name: yield
method.description: Identical to step()

method.name: setStepping
method.description:
Enables or disables the stepping operation mode for a threaded script. If enabled, then the current script has to trigger each simulation step explicitly, via step(). Is applied cumulatively, i.e. if the stepping operation mode is enabled n times, it needs to be disabled n times to return to the initial state
(Lua specific: in stepping operation mode, automatic thread interruptions, i.e. preemptive threading, is supressed)

param.name: enable
param.description: enable state of thread interruption

method.name: getStepping
method.description: Informs whether the stepping operation mode is enabled for the current script

param.name: enabled
param.description: the enabled state

method.name: lock
method.description: Acquires or releases a script lock, i.e. in order to forbid yielding or stepping

param.name: acquire
param.description: whether the lock should be acquired or released

method.name: registerFunctionHook
method.description: Registers a function hook for callback functions , that will be executed before or after the specified function

param.name: funcToHook
param.description: function to hook

param.name: userFunc
param.description: user function

param.name: execBefore
param.description: if true, the user function is called before the function to hook. Otherwise it is called after

method.name: removeFunctionHook
method.description: Removes a function hook

param.name: funcToHook
param.description: function name, where the hook is attached to

param.name: userFunc
param.description: user function

param.name: execBefore
param.description: true, if the function is called before the function to hook

method.name: remove
method.info: same method present in several classes
method.description: Removes the detached script. Currently only add-ons can be removed

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
noError (bool, defaults to false): errors are suppressed
delayed (bool, defaults to false): removal is delayed

method.name: callFunction
method.description: Calls a script function. This represents a user callback inside of a script. The target smust be initialized for this call to succeed, e.g. when calling from simulation scripts , then simulation must be running

param.name: functionName
param.description: the function to call.

param.name: inArg
param.description: argument to be handed over to the called function.

param.name: outArg
param.description: return from the called function.

method.name: getFunctions
method.description: Retrieves a map of the script's functions that can be called

param.name: object
param.description: a meta object. Functions can be called with object:functionName()

method.name: executeString
method.description: Executes some code from a string. The target must be initialized for this call to succeed, e.g. when calling from simulation scripts , then simulation must be running

param.name: stringToExecute
param.description: a string representing the code to execute in the specified script. An optional @python or @lua can be appended, to force a specific language

param.name: result
param.description: 0 or 1

param.name: executionResult
param.description: return value of the executed code

method.name: getStackTraceback
method.description: Retrieves and clears the last generated stack traceback for a script

param.name: stackTraceback
param.description: the stack traceback

method.name: init
method.description: Initializes/reinitializes a script. Operates in an asynchronous way when called upon the calling script

method.name: texture.setData
method.description: Overwrites a portion of the texture

param.name: data
param.description: RGB data corresponding to the portion of the texture to overwrite. A buffer size of 3 overwrites the portion of the texture with a single color

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
position (int[2], defaults to [0, 0]): position where to copy the RGB data. Set to [0, 0] to overwrite the full texture
size (int[2], defaults to [0, 0]): size of the RGB data. Set to [0, 0] to overwrite the full texture
interpolation (float, defaults to 0.0): fade or interpolation factor
rectangular (bool, defaults true): to write a rectangular or elliptical/circular portion of the texture

method.name: texture.getData
method.description: Reads a portion of the texture

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
position (int[2], defaults to [0, 0]): position where to read the RGB data. Set to [0, 0] to read the full texture
size (int[2], defaults to [0, 0]): size of the RGB data. Set to [0, 0] to read the full texture

param.name: data
param.description: RGB data

method.name: changeColor
method.info: same method present in collection and sceneObject
method.description: Changes the color of all shapes contained in the collection

param.name: color
param.description: The desired color

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
component (string, defaults to "diffuse"): possible values are "diffuse", "specular" and "emission"

param.name: originalColorData
param.description: Color data, to be used to restore the original color with restoreColor()

method.name: restoreColor
method.info: same method present in collection and sceneObject
method.description: Restores the color of all shapes contained in the collection

param.name: originalColorData
param.description: The original color data returned by changeColor()

method.name: remove
method.info: same method present in several classes
method.description: Removes the collection

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
noError (bool, defaults to false): errors are suppressed
delayed (bool, defaults to false): removal is delayed

method.name: addItem
method.description: Adds one or several items to this collection

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: a scene object

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
mode (int, defaults to sim.handle_single): operation mode. Following are allowed values: sim.handle_single (for a single object), sim.handle_all (for all objects in the scene), sim.handle_tree (for a tree of objects), sim.handle_chain (for a chain of objects (i.e. an inverted tree))
excludeObject (bool, defaults to false): if true, then the specified object is not included in the group of objects to add, if sim.handle_tree or sim.handle_chain is specified (i.e. the tree base or tip is excluded)

method.name: removeItem
method.description: Removes one or several items from this collection

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of a scene object

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
mode (int, defaults to sim.handle_single): operation mode. Following are allowed values: sim.handle_single (for a single object), sim.handle_all (for all objects in the scene), sim.handle_tree (for a tree of objects), sim.handle_chain (for a chain of objects (i.e. an inverted tree))
excludeObject (bool, defaults to false): if true, then the specified object is not included in the group of objects to remove, if sim.handle_tree or sim.handle_chain is specified (i.e. the tree base or tip is excluded)

method.name: checkCollision
method.description: Checks whether this collection collides with another collection or scene object. If the entity to check against is a scene object, then its collidable flag is ignored. If the entity to check against is the same collection, then a self-collision check is performed (in which case the collection self-collision indicators are taken into account)

param.name: entityHandle
param.description: handle of the other entity (can be a scene object or another collection), or sim.handle_all to check this collection against all other collidable scene objects

param.name: result
param.description: indicates the collision state

param.name: collidingObjectHandles
param.description: array containing the colliding scene object pair

method.name: checkDistance
method.description: Checks the minimum distance between this collection and another collection or scene object. If the entity to check against is a scene object, then its measurable flag is ignored. If the entity to check against is the same collection, then the minimum distance within this collection is computed

param.name: entityHandle
param.description: handle of the other entity (can be a scene object or another collection), or sim.handle_all to check this collection against all other measurable scene objects

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
threshold (float, defaults to 0.0): if distance is bigger than the threshold, the distance is not calculated and result is false

param.name: result
param.description: true if distance is smaller than the threshold

param.name: dist
param.description: distance

param.name: p1
param.description: point on entity 1

param.name: p2
param.description: point on entity 2

param.name: objectHandlePair
param.description: array of 2 object handles representing the two objects that hold the minimum distance segment

method.name: getContacts
method.description: Retrieves contact information from the dynamics world

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
dynPass (int, defaults to the last dynamic pass): by default, each simulation step runs the dynamics engine x times, i.e. executes sub-steps. At each of those sub-steps, contacts are created and destroyed. The dynPass parameter allows to focus on a particular sub-step, or on all sub-steps if dynPass is sim.handle_all

param.name: collisionPairs
param.description: pairs of colliding objects

param.name: points
param.description: contact points (matrix 3 * n)

param.name: forces
param.description: force vectors (matrix 3 * n)

param.name: normals
param.description: normal vectors (matrix 3 * n)

method.name: getDescendants
method.description: Retrieves scene objects up to a specific depth

param.name: objectTypes
param.description: the desired scene object types

param.name: depth
param.description: the retrieval depth

param.name: descendants
param.description: the retrieved descendant scene objects, ordered in a depth-first manner

method.name: groupShapes
method.description: Groups several shapes, and returns a compound shape

param.name: shapes
param.description: Shapes to be grouped

param.name: compoundShape
param.description: resulting compound shape

method.name: mergeShapes
method.description: Merges several shapes

param.name: shapes
param.description: Shapes to be merged. The last shape integrates the other shapes

param.name: resultingShape
param.description: resulting shape

method.name: getObjectFromUid
method.description: Retrieves an object based on its unique identifier

param.name: uid
param.description: the object unique identifier

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
noError (bool, defaults to false): generates a silent error if an object was not found

param.name: object
param.description: retrieved object

method.name: announceChange
method.description: Announces a change in the scene. This is required for the undo/redo function to operate properly when performing changes via the API. What this call will do is following: the whole scene will be serialized (saved) to memory as a scene image, and compared to a previously memorized scene image. Differences between both images are memorized. A call to this function has no effect (and doesn't generate any error) when called during simulation or when in edit mode.

method.name: getObject
method.description: Retrieves an object based on its path and name

param.name: objectPath
param.description: path and name of the object to find, or an object persistent unique id

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
noError (bool, defaults to false): generates a silent error if an object was not found
index (int, defaults to 0): fetches the n-th object (zero based index) that satisfies the specified objectPath

param.name: object
param.description: retrieved object

method.info: similar method present in both app and scene

param.description: scene filename. The filename extension is required

param.name: serializedScene

method.description: Closes the scene, and switches to another open scene. If there is no other open scene, a new scene is created
