method.info: same method present in both app and scene
method.description: Loads a previously saved model

param.name: filename
param.description: model filename. The filename extension is required (ttm, or simmodel.xml)

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
offset (vector3, defaults to [0.0, 0.0, 0.0]): position offset
assemblyDummy (handle, defaults to nil): dummy, onto which the model should be assembled

param.name: object
param.description: the model base scene object

param.name: info
param.description: information string

method.name: loadModelFromBuffer
method.info: same method present in both app and scene
method.description: Loads a model from buffer

param.name: buffer
param.description: a buffer containing the model

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
offset (vector3, defaults to [0.0, 0.0, 0.0]): position offset
assemblyDummy (handle, defaults to nil): dummy, onto which the model should be assembled

param.name: object
param.description: the model base scene object

param.name: info
param.description: information string

method.name: getObjects
method.info: same method present in both app and scene
method.description: Retrieves objects of a certain type

param.name: types
param.description: a string array specifying the types to retrieve. Allowed values are: "sceneObject", "shape", "joint", "dummy", "script", "mirror", "graph", "light", "camera", "marker", "proximitySensor", "visionSensor", "forceSensor", "ocTree", "pointCloud", "collection", "detachedScript", "mesh"

param.name: objects
param.description: the retrieved objects

method.name: removeObjects
method.info: same method present in both app and scene
method.description: Removes one or several objects

param.name: objects
param.description: objects to remove

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
noError (bool, defaults to false): errors are suppressed
delayed (bool, defaults to false): removal is delayed

method.name: duplicateObjects
method.description: Duplicates scene objects

param.name: objects
param.description: array of scene objects to duplicate

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
models (bool, defaults to false): whole models are copied. In that case, all specified scene objects should be flagged as model base
noScripts (bool, defaults to false): all scripts are stripped away
noCustomData (bool, defaults to false): all scene object custom data properties are stripped away
noObjectRefs (bool, defaults to false): all scene object references are stripped away
noTextures (bool, defaults to false): all textures are stripped away
noDna (bool, defaults to false): model tags and DNA information is stripped away

param.name: duplicates
param.description: the duplicate scene objects, where original and copy have the same location in their respective arrays

method.name: handleCustomizationScripts
method.description: Calls a system callback function in customization scripts . Customization scripts will be executed in a precise order . This function should only be called from the main script

param.name: callType
param.description: desired system call type (e.g. sysCall_actuation)

param.name: scriptCount
param.description: number of called scripts

method.name: handleSimulationScripts
method.description: Calls a system callback function in simulation scripts . Simulation scripts will be executed in a precise order . This function should only be called from the main script .

param.name: callType
param.description: the desired system call type (e.g. sysCall_actuation)

param.name: scriptCount
param.description: number of called simulation scripts

method.name: createObject
method.info: same method present in both app and scene
method.description:
Creates one of following objects:
collection
detached script
console
textEditor
dummy
joint
camera
light
marker
custom scene object
shape
script
force sensor
proximity sensor
vision sensor
oc tree
point cloud
path
graph

param.name: params
param.description:
a map/dict describing the object to create. All fields are optional except for objectType. Fields described hereafter are not exhaustive (all writable properties of the object to create can be specified too):
collection fields:
type (string): "collection"
override (bool): collidable, measurable, detectable and visibility properties of scene objects are overridden
detached script fields:
type (string): "detachedScript"
detachedScript.type (string): the script type. As of now only "addon" is supported
language (string): the script language ("lua" or "python")
code (string): the script content
console fields:
type (string): "console"
title (string): console title
size.width (int): console width
size.height (int): console height
position.x (int): console position x
position.y (int): console position y
closeable (bool): whether there is a close button or not
resizable (bool): whether the console can be resized
hiddenInSimulation (bool): whether the console is hidden during simulation
fontSize (int): the size of the font
defaultColor (color): text default color
background (color): background color
sceneUid (int): the scene unique identifier (sim.scene.uid) to which this console belongs to. Set to -1 for visibility across the whole application
textEditor fields:
type (string): "textEditor"
title (string): editor title
size.width (int): editor width
size.height (int): editor height
position.x (int): editor position x
position.y (int): editor position y
closeable (bool): whether there is a close button or not
resizable (bool): whether the editor can be resized
hiddenInSimulation (bool): whether the editor is hidden during simulation
fontSize (int): the size of the font
defaultColor (color): text default color
background (color): background color
sceneUid (int): the scene unique identifier (sim.scene.uid) to which this editor belongs to. Set to -1 for visibility across the whole application
dummy fields:
type (string): "dummy"
size (float): size of the dummy
color.diffuse (color): diffuse color
color.specular (color): specular color
color.emission (color): emission color
camera fields:
type (string): "camera"
clippingPlanes (float[2]): camera near and far clipping planes
size (float): camera size
viewAngle (float): the view angle, for a camera operating in perspective projection mode
viewSize (float): the view size, for a camera operating in orthogonal projection mode
color.diffuse (color): diffuse color
color.specular (color): specular color
color.emission (color): emission color
light fields:
type (string): "light"
light.type (string): the light type, any of "omnidirectional", "directional" or "spot"
attenuationFactors (float[3]): constant, linear and quadratic attenuation factors
spotCutoffAngle (float): spotlight cutoff angle
size (float): light size
lightColor.diffuse (color): light diffuse color
lightColor.specular (color): light specular color
lightColor.emission (color): light emission color
color.diffuse (color): object diffuse color
color.specular (color): object specular color
color.emission (color): object emission color
marker fields:
type (string): "marker"
marker.type (string): the marker object type. Allowed values are "points", "lines", "tubes", "triangles", "spheres", "squares", "discs", "cubes", "cylinders", "axes" or "custom"
cyclic (bool): if the marker is full, then the first items are overwritten
local (bool): coordinate data is specified locally
overlay (bool): items are rendered on top of scene objects
itemSize (vector3): default item size
duplicateTolerance (float): indicates a distance threshold under which points are not added. Does not apply to line- or triangle items
itemCnt (int): maximum item count
itemColor (color): default item color
mesh.vertices (matrix 3 * n): vertices for a mesh item type (sim.markertype_custom)
mesh.indices (int[]): indices for a mesh item type (sim.markertype_custom). One zero-based index for each vertex
mesh.normals (matrix 3 * n): normals for a mesh item type (sim.markertype_custom). One normal for each vertex
custom scene object fields:
type (string): "customSceneObject"
size (float): size of the custom scene object
color.diffuse (color): diffuse color
color.specular (color): specular color
color.emission (color): emission color
joint fields:
type (string): "joint"
joint.type (string): the joint type. Allowed values are "revolute", "prismatic" or "spherical"
mode (int): the joint mode
dynCtrlMode (int): the joint dynamic control mode
bounds (float[]): joint lower and upper bounds, [] for cyclic revolute joints, or for spherical joints
screwLead (float): a revolute joint with a screw lead different than 0.0 is a screw
length (float): length of the joint
diameter (float): diameter of the joint
color.diffuse (color): diffuse color
color.specular (color): specular color
color.emission (color): emission color
shape fields:
type (string): "shape"
shadingAngle (float): the shading angle
culling (bool): whether the shape has its back faces culled
dynamic (bool): whether the shape is dynamically enabled
showEdges (bool): whether the shape appears with visible edges
color.diffuse (color): diffuse color
color.specular (color): specular color
color.emission (color): emission color
cube (map/dict): holds parameters for the creation of a cube:
size (int[3]): the size of the cube
rawMesh (bool): whether the shape is registered as a random mesh (i.e. not a primitive)
sphere (map/dict): holds parameters for the creation of a sphere:
radius (float): the radius of the sphere
rawMesh (bool): whether the shape is registered as a random mesh (i.e. not a primitive)
cylinder (map/dict): holds parameters for the creation of a cylinder:
radius (float): the radius of the cylinder
length (float): the length of the cylinder
open (bool): whether the cylinder is open
rawMesh (bool): whether the shape is registered as a random mesh (i.e. not a primitive)
cone (map/dict): holds parameters for the creation of a cone:
radius (float): the radius of the cone
height (float): the height of the cone
open (bool): whether the cone is open
rawMesh (bool): whether the shape is registered as a random mesh (i.e. not a primitive)
capsule (map/dict): holds parameters for the creation of a capsule:
radius (float): the radius of the capsule
length (float): the length of the capsule
rawMesh (bool): whether the shape is registered as a random mesh (i.e. not a primitive)
plane (map/dict): holds parameters for the creation of a plane:
size (int[2]): the size of the plane
rawMesh (bool): whether the shape is registered as a random mesh (i.e. not a primitive)
disc (map/dict): holds parameters for the creation of a disc:
radius (float): the radius of the disc
rawMesh (bool): whether the shape is registered as a random mesh (i.e. not a primitive)
heightField (map/dict): holds parameters for the creation of a height field:
heights (matrix): the heights
cellSize (float): the length/width of a cell
rawMesh (bool): whether the shape is registered as a random mesh (i.e. not a height field)
string (map/dict): holds parameters for the creation of a shape representing a text string:
text (string): the text
height (float): the text height
center (bool): whether the text is centered or not
mesh (map/dict): holds parameters for the creation of a mesh:
vertices (matrix 3 * n)
indices (int[]): the zero-based indices
normals (matrix 3 * 3n)
boundingBoxQuaternion (quaternion): the orientation of the shape's bounding box
frameOrigin (pose): the pose of the shape's frame
texture (map/dict): holds parameters for an optional texture:
resolution (int[2]): texture resolution
image (buffer): texture specified as rgb or rgba
rgba (bool): whether the texture is specified as rgba or rgb
coordinates (matrix 2 * m): texture coordinates
interpolate (bool): whether adjecent texture pixels are interpolated
decal (bool): whether the texture is applied as decal
horizFlip (bool): whether the texture is horizontally flipped
vertFlip (bool): whether the texture is vertically flipped
script fields:
type (string): "script"
script.type (string): the script type. Allowed values are "simulation" and "customization"
language (string): "lua" or "python"
code (string): the content of the script
scriptDisabled (bool): the script is initially disabled
force sensors fields:
type (string): "forceSensor"
size (float): size of the sensor
filterType (int): filter type (0 = average, 1 = median)
filterSampleSize (int): value count the filter operates on
consecutiveViolationsToTrigger (int): number of consecutive threshold violation for the sensor to trigger
forceThreshold (float): force threshold
torqueThreshold (float): torque threshold
color.diffuse (color): diffuse color
color.specular (color): specular color
color.emission (color): emission color
proximity sensor fields:
type (string): "proximitySensor"
proximitySensor.type (string): the sensor type. Allowed values are "cone", "pyramid", "cylinder", "disc" and "ray"
pointSize (float): the sensor point size
explicitHandling (bool): whether the sensor is explicitly handled
showVolume (bool): whether the detection volume is shown
frontFaceDetection (bool): whether front faces are detected
backFaceDetection (bool): whether back faces are detected
exactMode (bool): whether the sensor operates in exact or fast mode
randomizedDetection (bool): randomized detection (only with ray-type proximity sensors)
angleThreshold (float): threshold angle for limited angle detection
closeThreshold (float): smallest detection distance
volume_faces (int[2]): near and far face count for the detection volume
volume_subdivisions (int[2]): near and far subdivisions for the detection volume
volume_offset (float): offset for the detection volume
volume_range (float): range for the detection volume
volume_xSize (float[2]): near and far x size for the detection volume
volume_ySize (float[2]): near and far y size for the detection volume
volume_radius (float[2]): near and far radius for the detection volume
volume_angle (float): angle for the detection volume
color.diffuse (color): diffuse color
color.specular (color): specular color
color.emission (color): emission color
vision sensor fields:
type (string): "visionSensor"
explicitHandling (bool): whether the sensor is explicitly handled
showFrustum (bool): whether the view frustum is shown
useExtImage (bool): whether the sensor is passive (uses an external image)
resolution (int[2]): sensor resolution
clippingPlanes (float[2]): sensor near and far clipping planes
size (float): sensor size
viewAngle (float): the view angle, for a sensor operating in perspective projection mode
viewSize (float): the view size, for a sensor operating in orthogonal projection mode
backgroundColor (color): the sensor uses a specific color as default background (i.e. null pixels)
color.diffuse (color): diffuse color
color.specular (color): specular color
color.emission (color): emission color
oc tree fields:
type (string): "ocTree"
voxelSize (float): the voxel size
showPoints (bool): whether points should be drawn instead of voxels
pointSize (int): the point size in pixels, when voxels are rendered as points
point cloud fields:
type (string): "pointCloud"
cellSize (float): the maximum size of the OC tree voxels containing points
pointSize (int): the point size in pixels
maxPointsInCell (int): maximum number of points allowed in a same OC tree voxel
path fields:
type (string): "path"
ctrlPts (matrix 7 * n): control points, with a pose for each path point
hiddenDuringSim (bool): path is hidden during simulation
closed (bool): last control point connects to the first control point
orientationMetric (float): the weight of orientation vs position
ctrlPoints: optional map describing control point properties:
duplicateThreshold (float): distance below which points are merged
linearityTolerance (float): points within a certain distance to the segment formed by its two neighbours can be discarded
lineType (enum, defaults to "line"): allowed values are "none", "line" and "tube"
lineColor (color): line color
pathPoints: optional map describing path point properties:
type (enum, defaults to "quadraticBezier"): allowed values are "linear" and "quadraticBezier"
bezierSmoothing (float, defaults to 1.0): the Bezier smoothing strength
samplingDistance (float): the maximum allowable spacing between consecutive points along the generated path
pointType (enum): allowed values are "none", "sphere" and "cube"
pointColor (color): point color
pointRadius (float): point radius
lineType (enum, defaults to "line"): allowed values are "none", "line" and "tube"
lineColor (color): line color
showAxes (bool): whether points display reference frames
extrusion: optional map describing how extrusion shapes are generated:
section (matrix 2 * n): the extrusion profile, with x/y pairs. If the first and last pair overlap, the section is closed
color (color): shape color
upVector (vector3, defaults to [0.0, 0.0, 1.0]): up-vector, used to generate the extruded shape
axis (int, defaults to 0): indicates what up-vector to use:
0: the upVector above
1: the upVector is the x-axis of path points
2: the upVector is the y-axis of path points
3: the upVector is the z-axis of path points
dummy: optional map describing how control point dummies are displayed:
size (float): the dummy size
color (color): the dummy color
visible (bool): whether the dummy is visible
graph fields:
type (string): "graph"
bufferSize (int): buffer size
graphSize (float): size of the graph's scene object
backgroundColor (color): background color
foregroundColor (color): foreground color

param.name: object
param.description: the created object

method.name: simulation.start
method.description: Requests a start of a simulation (or a resume of a paused simulation). Operation executes asynchronously

method.name: simulation.stop
method.description: Requests a simulation stop. Operation executes asynchronously

method.name: simulation.pause
method.description: Requests a simulation pause. Operation executes asynchronously

method.name: dynamics.step
method.description: Steps the dynamics engine

param.name: deltaTime
param.description: the simulation time step

method.name: makeObject
method.description: Instanciates a custom object from a class or template

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
appScope (bool, defaults to true): whether the custom object lives in sim.app, or in sim.scene
scriptPersistent (bool, defaults to false): whether the object survives the script that created it
volatile (bool, defaults to true): whether the object is serialized

param.name: object
param.description: The created custom object

method.name: remove
method.info: similar method present in several classes
method.description: Removes the custom scene object

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
noError (bool, defaults to false): errors are suppressed

method.name: makeObject
method.description: Instanciates a customized scene object from a class or template

param.name: object
param.description: The created customized scene object

method.name: makeClass
method.description: Creates a class or template from this object

param.name: className
param.description: The name of the class

param.name: class
param.description: The created class

method.name: changeColor
method.info: same method present in collection and sceneObject
method.description: Changes the color of the object, if it is a shape

param.name: color
param.description: The desired color

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
component (string, defaults to "diffuse"): possible values are "diffuse", "specular" and "emission"

param.name: originalColorData
param.description: Color data, to be used to restore the original color with restoreColor()

method.name: restoreColor
method.info: same method present in collection and sceneObject
method.description: Restores the color of the object, if it is a shape

param.name: originalColorData
param.description: The original color data returned by changeColor()

method.name: getObject
method.description: Retrieves an object in the current hierarchy tree, based on its path and name

param.name: objectPath
param.description: path and name of the object to find

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
noError (bool, defaults to false): generates a silent error if an object was not found
index (int, defaults to 0): fetches the n-th object (zero based index) that satisfies the specified objectPath

param.name: object
param.description: retrieved object

method.name: dynamicReset
method.description: Dynamically resets one or several objects that are dynamically simulated. This means that the object representation in the dynamics engine is removed, and added again. This can be useful when the set-up of a dynamically simulated chain needs to be modified during simulation (e.g. joint or shape attachement position/orientation changed)

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
tree (bool, defaults to false): set to true, in order to reset the object, together with its hierarchy tree

method.name: remove
method.info: similar method present in several classes
method.description: Removes the object from the scene

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
noError (bool, defaults to false): errors are suppressed
delayed (bool, defaults to false): removal is delayed

method.name: removeModel
method.description: Removes the model from the scene

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
noError (bool, defaults to false): errors are suppressed
delayed (bool, defaults to false): removal is delayed

method.name: saveModel
method.description: Saves this model. Any existing file with same name will be overwritten

param.name: filename
param.description: model filename. The filename extension is required (ttm or simmodel.xml)

method.name: saveModelToBuffer
method.description: Serializes this model to a buffer

param.name: modelData
param.description: a buffer containing the saved model

method.name: getDescendants
method.description: Retrieves descendant scene objects (children, grand-children, etc.)

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
types (string[], defaults to ["sceneObject"]): the desired scene object types
depth (int, defaults to 9999): the maximum retrieval depth

param.name: descendants
param.description: the retrieved descendant scene objects, ordered in a depth-first manner

method.name: getAncestors
method.description: Retrieves ancestor scene objects (parent, grand-parent, etc.)

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
types (string[], defaults to ["sceneObject"]): the desired scene object types
count (int, defaults to 9999): the maximum retrieval count

param.name: ancestors
param.description: the retrieved ancestor scene objects, ordered from near to far ancestors

method.name: visitTree
method.description: Executes a function on all objects of this object tree, in a depth-first manner

param.name: visitorFunction
param.description: the visitor function. Visitation ends if the function return false

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
types (string[], defaults to ["sceneObject"]): the desired scene object types to be taken into account

method.name: checkCollision
method.description: Checks whether this scene object collides with another scene object or a collection. The collidable flag of this object (and of the other entity if it is also a scene object) is ignored

param.name: entityHandle
param.description: handle of the other entity (can be a collection or another scene object), or sim.handle_all to check this scene object against all other collidable scene objects

param.name: result
param.description: indicates the collision state

param.name: collidingObjectHandles
param.description: array containing the colliding scene object pair

method.name: checkDistance
method.description: Checks the minimum distance between this scene object and another scene object or a collection. The measurable flag of this object (and of the other entity if it is also a scene object) is ignored

param.name: entityHandle
param.description: handle of the other entity (can be a collection or another scene object), or sim.handle_all to check this collection against all other measurable scene objects

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
threshold (float, defaults to 0.0): if distance is bigger than the threshold, the distance is not calculated and result is false

param.name: result
param.description: true if distance is smaller than the threshold

param.name: dist
param.description: distance

param.name: p1
param.description: point on entity 1

param.name: p2
param.description: point on entity 2

param.name: objectHandlePair
param.description: array of 2 object handles representing the two objects that hold the minimum distance segment

method.name: getName
method.description: Retrieves the name or path of a scene object

param.name: format
param.description:
name formating options:
"name": naked name (e.g. "name")
"nameOrder": ordered name (e.g. "name[0]")
"shortPath": short path that fully identifies the object (e.g. "/parent/name[0]")
"fullPath": full path (e.g. "/parentA/parentB/name[0]")
"nameHandle": naked name with handle (e.g. "name__42__")
"printpath": print version of the short path, not guaranteed to be unique (e.g. "/parent/ ... /name")
"nameIndex": naked name with index information (e.g. "name{3}")
"pathIndex": path with index information (e.g. "/parentModelA{4}/.../parentModelN{2}/name{3}")

param.name: decoratedName
param.description: name or path of the scene object

method.name: getPose
method.description: Retrieves the pose of a scene object

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
relativeToObject (handle, defaults to sim.handle_world): indicates relative to which reference frame we want the pose. Specify sim.handle_world to retrieve the absolute pose, sim.handle_inverse to retrieve the inverse of the absolute pose, sim.handle_parent to retrieve the pose relative to the object's parent, or an object relative to whose reference frame we want the pose
relativeToJointBase (bool, defaults to false): if relativeToObject is a joint, then the pose relative to the joint's moving frame is returned, unless this argument is true, in which case the pose relative to the joint's base frame is returned

param.name: pose
param.description: the pose: [x, y, z, qx, qy, qz, qw]

method.name: getPosition
method.description: Retrieves the position of a scene object

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
relativeToObject (handle, defaults to sim.handle_world): indicates relative to which reference frame we want the position. Specify sim.handle_world to retrieve the absolute position, sim.handle_inverse to retrieve the inverse of the absolute position, sim.handle_parent to retrieve the position relative to the object's parent, or an object relative to whose reference frame we want the position
relativeToJointBase (bool, defaults to false): if relativeToObject is a joint, then the position relative to the joint's moving frame is returned, unless this argument is true, in which case the position relative to the joint's base frame is returned

param.name: position
param.description: the position: [x, y, z]

method.name: getQuaternion
method.description: Retrieves the quaternion of a scene object

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
relativeToObject (handle, defaults to sim.handle_world): indicates relative to which reference frame we want the quaternion. Specify sim.handle_world to retrieve the absolute quaternion, sim.handle_inverse to retrieve the inverse of the absolute quaternion, sim.handle_parent to retrieve the quaternion relative to the object's parent, or an object relative to whose reference frame we want the quaternion
relativeToJointBase (bool, defaults to false): if relativeToObject is a joint, then the quaternion relative to the joint's moving frame is returned, unless this argument is true, in which case the quaternion relative to the joint's base frame is returned

param.name: quaternion
param.description: the quaternion: [qx, qy, qz, qw]

method.name: setPose
method.description: Sets the pose of a scene object. Dynamically simulated objects, together with their hierarchy tree, are dynamically reset (this however does not apply to static shapes)

param.name: pose
param.description: the pose: [x, y, z, qx, qy, qz, qw])

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
relativeToObject (handle, defaults to sim.handle_world): indicates relative to which reference frame the pose is specified. Specify sim.handle_world to set the absolute pose, sim.handle_inverse to set the inverse of the absolute pose, sim.handle_parent to set the pose relative to the scene object's parent, or a scene object relative to whose reference frame the pose is specified
relativeToJointBase (bool, defaults to false): if relativeToObject is a joint, then the pose relative to the joint's moving frame is set, unless this argument is true, in which case the pose relative to the joint's base frame is set

method.name: setPosition
method.description: Sets the position of a scene object. Dynamically simulated objects, together with their hierarchy tree, are dynamically reset (this however does not apply to static shapes)

param.name: position
param.description: the position: [x, y, z])

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
relativeToObject (handle, defaults to sim.handle_world): indicates relative to which reference frame the position is specified. Specify sim.handle_world to set the absolute position, sim.handle_inverse to set the inverse of the absolute position, sim.handle_parent to set the position relative to the scene object's parent, or a scene object relative to whose reference frame the position is specified
relativeToJointBase (bool, defaults to false): if relativeToObject is a joint, then the position relative to the joint's moving frame is set, unless this argument is true, in which case the position relative to the joint's base frame is set

method.name: setQuaternion
method.description: Sets the quaternion of a scene object. Dynamically simulated objects, together with their hierarchy tree, are dynamically reset (this however does not apply to static shapes)

param.name: quaternion
param.description: the quaternion: [qx, qy, qz, qw])

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
relativeToObject (handle, defaults to sim.handle_world): indicates relative to which reference frame the quaternion is specified. Specify sim.handle_world to set the absolute quaternion, sim.handle_inverse to set the inverse of the absolute quaternion, sim.handle_parent to set the quaternion relative to the scene object's parent, or a scene object relative to whose reference frame the quaternion is specified
relativeToJointBase (bool, defaults to false): is a joint, then the quaternion relative to the joint's moving frame is set, unless this argument is true, in which case the quaternion relative to the joint's base frame is set

method.name: setParent
method.description: Sets a scene object's parent. Dynamically simulated objects, together with their hierarchy tree, are dynamically reset (this however does not apply to static shapes)

param.name: parent
param.description: scene object that should become parent, or nil/None if the object should become an orphan. In an assembly operation, the assembly dummy on the parent should be specified instead

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
mode (string, defaults to "keepAbsolutePose"). Possible value are "keepRelativePose", "keepAbsolutePose" and "assembly"

method.name: scaleTree
method.description: Scales the specified scene object tree in an isometric fashion. All related values are automatically scaled appropriately (e.g. masses, etc.)

param.name: scalingFactor
param.description: scaling factor

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
scaleRootPosition (bool, defaults to true): if true, then the position of the root object is also scaled accordingly

method.name: scale
method.description: Scales the scene object in a non-isometric fashion, if possible. Some scene objects might have some constraints between their axes

param.name: scalingFactors
param.description: scaling factors along the object's x, y and z-axis

method.name: addItems
method.description: Adds items to a marker

param.name: points
param.description: points, organised column-wise. If the items are point items, the matrix should be 3 * n in size. If the items are line items, the matrix should be 3 * 2n in size, etc

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
colors (matrix 4 * n, defaults to nil): point-specific colors (one color per point)
quaternions (matrix 4 * n, defaults to nil): quaternions (one quaternion per item)
sizes (matrix 3 * n, defaults to nil): x/y/z sizes (one x/y/z per item)

param.name: ids
param.description: item-specific ids (one id per item)

method.name: clearItems
method.description: Clears a marker

method.name: removeItems
method.description: Removes specific items from a marker

param.name: ids
param.description: item-specific ids (one id per item to remove)

method.name: getContacts
method.info: same method present in both scene and shape
method.description: Retrieves this shape's contact information from the dynamics world

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
dynPass (int, defaults to the last dynamic pass): by default, each simulation step runs the dynamics engine x times, i.e. executes sub-steps. At each of those sub-steps, contacts are created and destroyed. The dynPass parameter allows to focus on a particular sub-step, or on all sub-steps if dynPass is sim.handle_all

param.name: collisionPairs
param.description: pairs of colliding objects

param.name: points
param.description: contact points (matrix 3 * n)

param.name: forces
param.description: force vectors (matrix 3 * n)

param.name: normals
param.description: normal vectors (matrix 3 * n)

method.name: getAppearance
method.description: Fetches the shape's main properties related to appearance

param.name: appearanceData
param.description: Appearance data

method.name: setAppearance
method.description: Sets the shape's main properties related to appearance

param.name: appearanceData
param.description: Appearance data

method.name: ungroup
method.description: Ungroups (decomposes) a compound shape

param.name: shapes
param.description: array holding the ungrouped shapes

method.name: divide
method.description: Divides a simple shape into shapes that do not share common vertices. A compound shape should be ungrouped before being divided

param.name: shapes
param.description: array holding the divides shapes

method.name: addForce
method.description: Applies a force to the dynamically enabled shape. Forces are added cumulatively

param.name: force
param.description: force to add

param.name: offset
param.description: optional offset (relative to the shape center of mass) where to apply the force: induces a torque when non-zero

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
reset (bool, defaults to false): clears cumulative forces before applying the new force
relative (bool, defaults to false): applies the force/offset relative to the shape's reference frame axes, otherwise relative to the world

method.name: addTorque
method.description: Applies a torque to the dynamically enabled shape. Torque are added cumulatively

param.name: torque
param.description: torque to add

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
reset (bool, defaults to false): clears cumulative torques before applying the new torque
relative (bool, defaults to false): applies the torque relative to the shape's reference frame axes, otherwise relative to the world

method.name: computeInertia
method.description: Computes and applies mass and inertia properties to the shape, based on its convex representation

param.name: density
param.description: density expressed in kg/m^3

method.name: setInertia
method.description: Applies a new inertia matrix

param.name: inertia
param.description: new inertia (matrix 3 * 3), expressed relative to comPose (itself expressed relative to the shape's reference frame)

param.name: comPose
param.description: a pose expressed relative to the shape's reference frame. The pose indicates the center of mass of the shape, and is the frame relative to which inertiaMatrix is expressed

method.name: getInertia
method.description: Retrieves the shape's inertia information

param.name: inertia
param.description: inertia (matrix 3 * 3), expressed relative to the shape's reference frame orientation. The matrix is symmetric

param.name: com
param.description: the center of mass, expressed relative to the shape's reference frame

method.name: relocateFrame
method.description: Repositions and reorients the shape's reference frame, while keeping the mesh in place. The shape's inertia properties are unaffected

param.name: pose
param.description: desired pose for the shape's reference frame. Omitting this argument relocates the frame to the center of the mesh's bounding box

method.name: alignBoundingBox
method.description: Reorients the shape's bounding box, while keeping the shape frame in place. The shape's inertia properties are unaffected

param.name: quaternion
param.description: quaternion describing the desired orientation of the shape's bounding box. Omitting this argument aligns the bounding box with the mesh's natural bounding box

method.name: checkSensor
method.description: Checks whether this proximity sensor detects the indicated entity. Detection is silent (no visual feedback) and the detectable flags of the entity to detect are overridden, if the entity is a scene object

param.name: entity
param.description: entity to detect (scene object or collection), or sim.handle_all to detect all detectable objects

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
frontFaces (bool, defaults to sensor's settings): front faces are detected
backFaces (bool, defaults to sensor's settings): back faces are detected
exact (bool, defaults to sensor's settings): exact mode, otherwise approximate mode (a bit faster)
maxNormal (float, defaults to sensor's settings): the maximum angle between surface normal and detection ray

param.name: result
param.description: detection state

param.name: dist
param.description: distance from the sensor origin to the detected point

param.name: point
param.description: position of the detected point relative to the sensor reference frame

param.name: detectedObject
param.description: detected object

param.name: n
param.description: normal vector of the surface where the point was detected. Relative to the sensor reference frame

method.name: handleSensor
method.description: Handles this proximity sensor, i.e. does detection. Detection is not silent (i.e. there is visual feedback), and the detectable flags of the scene objects to detect are taken into account

param.name: result
param.description: detection state

param.name: dist
param.description: distance from the sensor origin to the detected point

param.name: point
param.description: position of the detected point relative to the sensor reference frame

param.name: detectedObject
param.description: detected object

param.name: n
param.description: normal vector of the surface where the point was detected. Relative to the sensor reference frame

method.name: resetSensor
method.description: Clears the detection state and visual feedback of this proximity sensor

method.name: getDepth
method.description: Reads the vision sensor's depth buffer (or a portion of it)

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
position (int[2], defaults to [0, 0]): position of the image portion to write
size (int[2], defaults to [0, 0]): size of the image portion to write. [0, 0] corresponds to [resolutionX, resolution Y]

param.name: depth
param.description: depth values in meters (matrix y * x)

param.name: resolution
param.description: the vision sensor resolution

method.name: getImage
method.description: Reads the vision sensor's image (or a portion of it)

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
position (int[2], defaults to [0, 0]): position of the image portion to retrieve
size (int[2], defaults to [0, 0]): size of the image portion to retrieve. [0, 0] corresponds to [resolutionX, resolution Y]
type (string, defaults to "rgb"): the type of the returned image. Valid values are "rgb", "rgba" and "grey"
rgbaCutOff (float, defaults to 0.0): when a RGBA image is returned, the alpha component is 255 for all depth values below rgbaCutOff, and 0 for all depth values above rgbaCutOff. 0.0 corresponds to the near clipping plane, 1.0 to the far clipping plane

param.name: image
param.description: the returned image

param.name: resolution
param.description: the vision sensor resolution

method.name: setImage

param.name: image
param.description: image to write

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
position (int[2], defaults to [0, 0]): position of the image portion to write
size (int[2], defaults to [0, 0]): size of the image portion to write. [0, 0] corresponds to [resolutionX, resolution Y]

method.name: checkSensor
method.description: Checks whether the vision sensor detects the indicated entity. Detection is silent. The vision callback functions are called on the acquired image. Also, the visibility state of the entity is overridden if the entity is an object

param.name: entityHandle
param.description: handle of entity to detect (object or collection), or sim.handle_all to detect all detectable objects

param.name: detection
param.description: detection state

param.name: auxPacket1
param.description: packet of 15 auxiliary values: the minimum of intensity, red, green, blue, depth value, the maximum of intensity, red, green, blue, depth value, and the average of intensity, red, green, blue, depth value

param.name: auxPacket2
param.description: additional auxiliary value packet (e.g. from an image processing component)

method.name: handleSensor
method.description: Handles (performs sensing, etc. of) the vision sensor object. It (1) clear previous computed image processing data, (2) reads an image and (3) performs image processing via the vision callback functions (if the vision sensor is using an external input only (1) is performed)

param.name: detection
param.description: detection state

param.name: packet1
param.description: default auxiliary value packet (15 auxiliary values: the minimum of [intensity red green blue depth], the maximum of [intensity red green blue depth], and the average of [intensity red green blue depth])

param.name: packet2
param.description: additional auxiliary value packet (e.g. from an image processing component)

method.name: resetSensor
method.description: Clears the detection state, etc. of the vision sensor

method.name: getData
method.description: Returns similar data as the one acquired by the camera

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
resolution (int[2], defaults to [1920, 1080]): desired resolution
clippingPlanes (float[2], defaults to [0.05, 30.0]): near and far clipping planes
renderMode (string, defaults to "openGl"): valid values are "openGl", "colorCoded", "povray", "extRenderer", "openGl3", "oglImg", "codedImg"
viewAngle (float, defaults to 60 degrees): view angle. When specified, then the camera is in perspective projection mode
viewSize (float, defaults to nil): view size. When specified, then the camera is in orthogonal projection mode

param.name: image
param.description: the image as a succession of RGB values

param.name: packedDepths
param.description: the depth buffer in meters, packed as float values

param.name: resolution
param.description: the resolution

method.name: addSignal
method.description: Adds or updates a graph signal

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
name (string, defaults to "signal"): signal name
color (color, defaults to Color("red")): signal color
unit (string, defaults to ""): signal unit
hideSignal (bool, defaults to false): hides the signal
hideLabel (bool, defaults to false): hides the label
drawLine (bool, defaults to false): draws a line
cyclicRange (float, defaults to 0.0): can be used with cyclic values (e.g. angles) for correct data interpretation during stream data transformation
transformation (map): signal transformation options:
type (string, defaults to "raw"): transformation type: "raw", "derivative", "integral" or "cumulative"
scale (float, defaults to 1.0): scaling
offset (float, defaults to 0.0): offset
smoothing (int, defaults to 1): the moving average period. Set to 1 for no moving average

param.name: signalId
param.description: id of the created/updated signal

method.name: addCurve
method.description: Adds or updates a graph curve

param.name: signals
param.description: signal ids. -1 for a missing signal, in which case the default value is used

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
color (color, defaults to Color("green")): curve color
name (string, defaults to "curve"): curve name
unit (string, defaults to ""): curve unit
default (float[2], defaults to [0.0, 0.0]): default signal values
width (int, defaults to 2): curve width
hideLabel (bool, defaults to false): hides the label
drawLine (bool, defaults to false): draws a line

param.name: curveId
param.description: id of the created/updated curve

method.name: reset
method.description: Resets the graph, i.e. clears all its signals

method.name: removeTrace
method.description: Removes a signal or curve

param.name: curveId
param.description: id of the signal or curve, -1 to remove all signals/curves

method.name: setSignalPoint
method.description: Sets the next signal value to be recorded

param.name: signalId
param.description: id of the signal

param.name: point
param.description: value to set. When omitting to set a value for a corresponding graph:step() call, then the data point will be missing

method.name: step
method.description: Records another value for each signal, given that such value was provided via graph:setSignalPoint()

param.name: time
param.description: time where next value will be recorded at, usually the simulation time

method.name: snapshotTrace
method.description: Duplicates a graph trace, and freezes it

param.name: traceId
param.description: trace id

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
color (color, defaults to Color("white")): trace color
name (string, defaults to "snapshot"): trace name

param.name: staticTraceId
param.description: static trace id

method.name: addFromObjects
method.info: similar method present in both ocTree and pointCloud
method.description: Adds voxels from scene objects

param.name: objects
param.description: List of scene objects, where only shapes, dummies, point clouds and oc-trees are considered

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
color (color, defaults to the oc tree's default): voxel color
tag (int, defaults to 0): voxel tag, i.e. a user defined value

method.name: subtractFromObjects
method.info: similar method present in both ocTree and pointCloud
method.description: Subtracts voxels from scene objects

param.name: objects
param.description: List of scene objects, where only shapes, dummies, point clouds and oc-trees are considered

method.name: addVoxels
method.info: similar method present in both ocTree and pointCloud
method.description: Inserts voxels

param.name: voxels
param.description: voxel positions (matrix 3 * n)

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
color (color, defaults to nil): color for all voxels
colors (matrix 4 * n, defaults to nil): voxel-specific colors (one color per voxel)
tag (int, defaults to 0): tag for all voxels
tags (int[], defaults to nil): voxel-specific tags (one tag per voxel)
relative (bool, defaults to false): specified voxels are relative to the OC tree reference frame, otherwise they are relative to the world reference frame

method.name: addPackedVoxels
method.info: similar method present in both ocTree and pointCloud
method.description: Inserts voxels from a buffer, instead from an array

param.name: voxels
param.description: voxel positions, packed as a float array

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
color (color, defaults to nil): color for all voxels
colors (buffer, defaults to nil): voxel-specific colors, packed as a float array (one color per voxel)
tag (int, defaults to 0): tag for all voxels
tags (buffer, defaults to nil): voxel-specific tags, packed as an unsigned float array (one tag per voxel)
relative (bool, defaults to false): specified voxels are relative to the OC tree reference frame, otherwise they are relative to the world reference frame

method.name: subtractVoxels
method.description: Removes voxels

param.name: voxels
param.description: voxel positions (matrix 3 * n)

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
relative (bool, defaults to false): specified voxels are relative to the OC tree reference frame, otherwise they are relative to the world reference frame

method.name: subtractPackedVoxels
method.description: Removes voxels from a buffer, instead from an array

param.name: voxels
param.description: voxel positions, packed as a float array

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
relative (bool, defaults to false): specified voxels are relative to the OC tree reference frame, otherwise they are relative to the world reference frame

method.name: checkPoints
method.description: Checks whether the provided points collide with the voxels

param.name: points
param.description: point positions (matrix 3 * n)

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
relative (bool, defaults to false): specified points are relative to the OC tree reference frame, otherwise they are relative to the world reference frame

param.name: result
param.description: whether the points collide with the voxels

param.name: tag
param.description: tag value of the voxel that collides with a single point. If several points are tested, this return value should be ignored

param.name: locLow
param.description: low value specifying the location of the voxel that collides with a single point. If several points are tested, then this return value should be ignored

param.name: locHigh
param.description: high value specifying the location of the voxel that collides with a single point. If several points are tested, then this return value should be ignored

method.name: clear
method.info: similar method present in both ocTree and pointCloud
method.description: Removes all voxels

method.name: addFromObjects
method.info: similar method present in both ocTree and pointCloud
method.description: Adds points from scene objects

param.name: objects
param.description: List of scene objects, where only shapes, dummies, point clouds and oc-trees are considered

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
color (color, defaults to the point cloud's default): point color
gridSize (float, defaults to 0.02): when a shape is inserted, it will first be converted to an OC tree with the specified grid/voxel size
duplicateTolerance (float, defaults to the point cloud's default): a minimum distance value used to avoid duplicate points

method.name: subtractFromObjects
method.info: similar method present in both ocTree and pointCloud
method.description: Subtracts points from scene objects

param.name: objects
param.description: List of scene objects, where only dummies, point clouds and oc-trees are considered

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
tolerance (float, defaults to 0.02): a distance tolerance value

method.name: addPoints
method.description: Inserts points

param.name: points
param.description: point positions (matrix 3 * n)

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
color (color, defaults to nil): color for all points
colors (matrix 4 * n, defaults to nil): point-specific colors (one color per point)
relative (bool, defaults to false): specified points are relative to the point cloud reference frame, otherwise they are relative to the world reference frame
tolerance (float, defaults to nil): an insertion duplicate tolerance

method.name: addPackedPoints
method.description: Inserts points from a buffer, instead from an array

param.name: points
param.description: point positions, packed as a float array

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
color (color, defaults to nil): color for all points
colors (buffer, defaults to nil): point-specific colors, packed as a float array (one color per point)
relative (bool, defaults to false): specified points are relative to the point cloud reference frame, otherwise they are relative to the world reference frame
tolerance (float, defaults to nil): an insertion duplicate tolerance

method.name: subtractPoints
method.description: Removes points

param.name: points
param.description: point positions (matrix 3 * n)

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
relative (bool, defaults to false): specified points are relative to the point cloud reference frame, otherwise they are relative to the world reference frame
tolerance (float, defaults to 0.01): an insertion duplicate tolerance

method.name: subtractPackedPoints
method.description: Removes points from a buffer, instead from an array

param.name: points
param.description: point positions, packed as a float array

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
relative (bool, defaults to false): specified points are relative to the point cloud reference frame, otherwise they are relative to the world reference frame
tolerance (float, defaults to 0.01): an insertion duplicate tolerance

method.name: intersectPoints
method.description: Removes points that do not intersect with the provided points (i.e. the result in the point cloud will be the intersection between the two sets of points)

param.name: points
param.description: point positions (matrix 3 * n)

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
relative (bool, defaults to false): specified points are relative to the point cloud reference frame, otherwise they are relative to the world reference frame
tolerance (float, defaults to 0.01): a distance tolerance

method.name: clear
method.info: similar method present in both ocTree and pointCloud
method.description: Removes all points

method.name: setTargetPosition
method.description: Sets the joints's desired linear/angular position (only for non-spherical joints). When in kinematic mode, the joint moves according to a motion profile that respects maximum velocity, acceleration and jerk values. In dynamic and position/custom control mode, the controller is instructed about the desired position

param.name: targetPosition
param.description: desired position

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
maxVelocity (float, defaults to joint's setting): the maximum allowed velocity
maxAcceleration (float, defaults to joint's setting): the maximum allowed acceleration
maxJerk (float, defaults to joint's setting): the maximum allowed jerk

method.name: setTargetVelocity
method.description: Sets the joint's desired linear/angular velocity (only for non-spherical joints). When in kinematic mode, the joint moves according to a motion profile that respects maximum acceleration and jerk values. In dynamic and velocity control mode, the controller is instructed about the desired velocity

param.name: targetVelocity
param.description: desired velocity

param.name: options
param.description:
optional map:
initVel (float, defaults to joint's current velocity): the initial velocity, when in kinematic mode
maxAcceleration (float, defaults to joint's setting): the maximum allowed acceleration
maxJerk (float, defaults to joint's setting): the maximum allowed jerk

method.name: getClosestPosOnPath
method.description: Returns the position or distance along a path that is closest to a specified point in space

param.name: path
param.description: path, specified in row-major order, with x/y/z values for each path point

param.name: pathLengths
param.description: lengths of a path. Each path point should have a corresponding length value (as the distance from the path's first point, along the path). See also sim.getPathLengths .

param.name: absPt
param.description: point in 3D space

param.name: posAlongPath
param.description: position/distance along the path
